<div align="center">

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="AI Screen Assistant banner" width="100%" />

# 📸 AI Screen Assistant

**Press a shortcut. Capture your screen. Ask AI anything about it.**
No new tab, no copy-pasting screenshots into a chat window — just a floating answer, right where you're looking.

![Manifest V3](https://img.shields.io/badge/Manifest-V3-4fb0a5?style=flat-square)
![Chrome](https://img.shields.io/badge/Chrome-supported-e8a33d?style=flat-square&logo=googlechrome&logoColor=white)
![Firefox](https://img.shields.io/badge/Firefox-supported-e8a33d?style=flat-square&logo=firefoxbrowser&logoColor=white)
![Providers](https://img.shields.io/badge/AI%20Providers-6-4fb0a5?style=flat-square)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-9298b5?style=flat-square)

</div>

---

## What is this, exactly?

You're staring at a cryptic error message, a chart you don't understand, a code snippet you didn't write, or a form in a language you don't speak. Instead of screenshotting it, switching to ChatGPT, uploading the image, and typing "what is this" — you just press **`Ctrl+Shift+A`**.

That's it. The extension grabs what's on your screen, sends it to whichever AI provider you've configured, and a clean little card appears with the answer, right on top of the page you were already looking at.

<div align="center">
<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Overlay UI mockup showing a code explanation with syntax highlighting and a follow-up input" width="640" />

<sub>What the overlay looks like — Markdown formatting, syntax-highlighted code, a follow-up box, all in a draggable card that stays out of your way.</sub>
</div>

---

## ✨ Features

| | |
|---|---|
| 🖱️ **One shortcut, done** | `Ctrl+Shift+A` captures and analyzes in one motion — no menus. |
| 🤖 **Bring your own AI** | OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, or DeepSeek — your choice, your key. |
| 🔍 **Auto-detects your provider** | Paste any key into Settings and it recognizes which company it belongs to and configures itself. |
| 🪟 **Floating overlay, not a tab** | Markdown rendering, syntax-highlighted code, copy buttons, draggable, resizable. |
| 🙈 **Alt+Z hides/shows it** | Instantly tuck it away without losing your result, then bring it right back. |
| 💬 **Follow-up questions** | Ask a second question about the same screenshot without recapturing. |
| 🌓 **Dark & light themes** | Matches your system, or set it manually. |
| 🕘 **Local history** | Every capture is saved, searchable, and reopenable — never synced anywhere. |
| 🔒 **Privacy-first by design** | API keys never touch a web page's JavaScript — only the extension's private background process. |
| 🪶 **No bloated dependencies** | Markdown rendering and syntax highlighting are hand-written, not bundled from a CDN. |

---

## 🔄 How it works

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant You
    participant Overlay as Floating Overlay
    participant BG as Background Worker
    participant AI as AI Provider

    You->>BG: Press Ctrl+Shift+A
    BG->>Overlay: Inject + show "Capturing…"
    BG->>BG: Screenshot the visible tab
    BG->>AI: Send image + your question
    AI-->>BG: Analysis (text)
    BG->>Overlay: Render Markdown answer
    You->>Overlay: (optional) Ask a follow-up
    Overlay->>BG: Follow-up question
    BG->>AI: Same image + new question
    AI-->>Overlay: New answer
```

Nothing about your API key ever passes through the web page you're looking at — every network call happens inside the extension's isolated background worker.

---

## 🧠 Supported AI providers

| Provider | Free tier? | Card needed? | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|
| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Yes (`:free` models) | ❌ No | Easiest zero-cost path to a real vision model |
| **Groq** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | Very fast responses |
| **Google Gemini** | ✅ Yes (Flash models) | ❌ No | Generous free daily quota |
| **OpenAI** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | GPT-4o class quality |
| **Anthropic (Claude)** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | Claude's reasoning quality |
| **DeepSeek** | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Yes | If you already use their ecosystem |

> 💡 Free tiers and included models shift over time as providers change their offerings — if a model in Settings stops working, that's usually why. Just check the provider's current docs and pick a replacement from the dropdown.

---

## 🌐 Install in Chrome

1. Unzip the extension folder somewhere permanent — **don't delete it later**, Chrome loads the extension live from that folder.
2. Open a new tab and go to `chrome://extensions`.
3. Flip on **Developer mode** in the top-right corner.
4. Click **Load unpacked**.
5. Select the `ai-screen-assistant` folder (the one that directly contains `manifest.json`).
6. Done — the icon appears in your toolbar. Click the puzzle-piece icon next to the address bar and pin it for one-click access.

**Updating later?** Replace the folder's contents with a newer version, then click the reload ⟳ icon on the extension's card in `chrome://extensions`.

---

## 🦊 Install in Firefox

1. Open a new tab and go to `about:debugging#/runtime/this-firefox`.
2. Click **Load Temporary Add-on…**.
3. Select the `manifest.json` file inside the `ai-screen-assistant` folder.
4. Done — the icon appears in your toolbar.

> ⚠️ **Heads up:** this load method is *temporary* — Firefox removes it when you restart the browser. That's a Firefox rule for unsigned extensions, not a bug. For a permanent install, the extension needs to be signed via [addons.mozilla.org](https://addons.mozilla.org/developers/), or run continuously with the `web-ext run` developer tool. For everyday testing, just re-load it after restarting Firefox — it takes ten seconds.

**Updating later?** Remove the old temporary add-on entry, then **Load Temporary Add-on…** again on the new `manifest.json`.

---

## 🔑 First-time setup

1. Click the toolbar icon → **Settings** (or right-click the icon → **Options** / **Manage Extension**).
2. You'll land on the **AI Provider** tab.
3. Get a free API key from whichever provider you want — the fastest path is **OpenRouter** (`openrouter.ai/keys`, no card required).
4. **Paste the key into the API key field.** Watch the page — it auto-detects which provider the key belongs to and switches the dropdowns to match.
5. Click **Test key** — it'll tell you immediately whether it's valid.
6. Click **Save**.

That's the whole setup. You're ready to go.

---

## 🚀 Using it day to day

1. Land on any regular webpage — an article, some code, a spreadsheet, anything visible on screen.
2. Press **`Ctrl+Shift+A`** (`Cmd+Shift+A` on Mac).
3. Watch the overlay walk through *Capturing → Uploading → Analyzing → Rendering* and then show the answer.
4. Got a follow-up? Type it into the box at the bottom of the card and hit **Ask** — no need to recapture, it reuses the same screenshot.
5. Done looking? Press **`Esc`**, or click the ✕.

Prefer to type a specific question before capturing? Click the toolbar icon instead of using the shortcut — there's a text box there for exactly that.

---

## ⌨️ All keyboard shortcuts

| Shortcut | What it does |
|---|---|
| `Ctrl+Shift+A` (`Cmd+Shift+A` on Mac) | Capture the visible tab and analyze it |
| `Ctrl+Shift+U` (`Cmd+Shift+U` on Mac) | Open the toolbar popup |
| `Alt+Z` | Hide/show the overlay (keeps your result and draft intact) |
| `Esc` | Close the overlay completely |

> Shortcuts are managed by the browser, not the extension — see below if you need to change them.

**Changing the capture shortcut:**
- Chrome: go to `chrome://extensions/shortcuts`
- Firefox: go to `about:addons` → gear icon → **Manage Extension Shortcuts**

(Browsers deliberately don't let extensions change this programmatically — it's a security boundary, not a limitation of this extension. The Settings page's **Keyboard Shortcut** tab links straight to the right place.)

---

## ⚙️ Settings, tab by tab

**AI Provider** — pick a provider and model, paste/test/save your API key.

**Popup Appearance** — where the overlay appears on screen (corner or center), how wide it is, font size, and dark/light/auto theme.

**Keyboard Shortcut** — shows your current capture shortcut and links to where you can change it.

**History** — every past capture, searchable by keyword, reopenable in full, deletable one at a time or all at once. Stored only on this device.

**About & Privacy** — a plain-language summary of exactly what data goes where.

---

## 🔒 Privacy — what actually leaves your browser

- Your API key lives **only** in this browser's local extension storage. It is never synced to an account, never sent to Anthropic, Google, or anywhere else that isn't the AI provider you personally configured.
- Screenshots go **directly** from your browser to your chosen provider's official API over HTTPS. There is no middleman server run by this extension.
- All AI requests happen inside the background service worker — a part of the browser a web page's own JavaScript can never read from. A malicious or compromised site cannot see your API key, ever.
- History is stored locally and only locally. Clear it anytime from Settings → History → **Clear all**.

---

## 📁 Project structure

```
ai-screen-assistant/
├─ manifest.json              # Extension configuration (MV3, Chrome + Firefox)
├─ background/                # Service worker — orchestrates capture → AI → overlay
├─ content/                   # Injected on demand, builds the floating overlay
├─ overlay/                   # Overlay visual styling
├─ options/                   # Settings page
├─ popup/                     # Toolbar popup
├─ services/                  # Provider dispatch logic
├─ providers/                 # One file per AI provider (openai, anthropic, gemini…)
├─ storage/                   # Settings + history persistence
├─ utils/                     # Shared helpers (Markdown renderer, syntax highlighter, etc.)
└─ assets/icons/               # Extension icons
```

---

## 🛠️ Troubleshooting

<details>
<summary><strong>"Could not capture this tab"</strong></summary>

Some pages block screenshots by design — browser internal pages (`chrome://…`, `about:…`), extension store pages, and some PDF viewers. Try a regular website instead.
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>"No API key set" / "rejected the API key"</strong></summary>

Open Settings → AI Provider, confirm the provider selected matches the one you added a key for, and use **Test key** to double-check it's valid before saving.
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>The overlay never appears</strong></summary>

Open `chrome://extensions` (or `about:debugging` in Firefox), find the extension, and click **Inspect views: service worker** to see console errors. Also confirm the keyboard shortcut isn't already claimed by another extension.
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Firefox: the extension disappeared after a restart</strong></summary>

Expected behavior for temporary installs — see the Firefox install steps above. Just reload it, or sign it for a permanent install.
</details>

---

<div align="center">
<sub>Built with official WebExtensions APIs only. No trackers, no ads, no telemetry.</sub>
</div>
